popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Count of Loos and Rieneck; Burggraf of Mainz, 1138-1162.  Advocate of Averboden and Munsterbilsen, 1155.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/LOTHARINGIAN%20(LOWER)%20NOBILITY.htm#LudwigILoo …

zh.Erfenbach, Kaiserslautern, Pfalz, Bayern: 德國貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lodewijk I van Loon於大約1110年出生時，他的父親Arnold II van Loon32歲，母親Adelheid27歲。他於1140年與Agnes de Metz在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1171年8月11日在比利時利包格波格洛享年62歲，埋葬在比利時利包格波格洛。

zh-hans.Erfenbach, Kaiserslautern, Pfalz, Bayern: 德国貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lodewijk I van Loon于大约1110年出生时，他的父亲Arnold II van Loon32岁，母亲Adelheid27岁。他于1140年与Agnes de Metz在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 凱澤斯勞特艾分巴賀结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1171年8月11日在比利时利包格波格洛享年62岁，埋葬在比利时利包格波格洛。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Loon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lon .
possibly a variant of English Lund , which is either a topographic name from Old Norse lundr ‘grove’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word.

variants.name: Lon
meanings: English: probably a variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lown .
Cambodian: written លន់, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘(to be) superb’. Compare   Lun   4.

Burmese (Shan): from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, of unexplained etymology. Compare   Lone   4. — Note: Since Shans do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Lowndes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lownes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lundy
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places called Lundie, for example one near Doune in Perthshire.
Irish: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French de la Launde ‘of the glade’. Compare Lown 1.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Lunde  .

nameMeanings.name: Lodewijk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

variants.name: Lewie
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Louis  , or a pet form of   Lewis .

variants.name: Louie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Lewie   or   Louis  , currently in fashion.

variants.name: Lewi
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

en.lifeSummary: When Lodewijk I van Loon was born about 1110, his father, Arnold II van Loon, was 32 and his mother, Adelheid, was 27. He married Agnes de Metz in 1140, in Erfenbach, Kaiserslautern, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 11 August 1171, in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium, at the age of 62, and was buried in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lodewijk I van Loon est né environ 1110, son père, Arnold II van Loon, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Adelheid, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes de Metz en 1140, à Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 11 août 1171, à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lodewijk I van Loon um 1110 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Arnold II van Loon 32 und seine Mutter Adelheid 27. Er hat 1140 in Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Deutschland Agnes de Metz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 11. August 1171 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien und wurde in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lodewijk I van Loon è nato il circa 1110, il suo padre, Arnold II van Loon, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Adelheid, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes de Metz nel 1140, nel Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 11 agosto 1171, nel Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Lodewijk I van Loonが約1110年に生まれた時，父Arnold II van Loonは32歳，母Adelheidは27歳であった。1140年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ケイセルスラウテルンエルフエンバチでAgnes de Metzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1171年8月11日にベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンで62歳で死去し，ベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lodewijk I van Loon(이)가 대략 1110년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Arnold II van Loon(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelheid(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1140년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 카이세르슬로터 르펜바치에서 Agnes de Metz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1171년 8월 11일 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에 묻혔다.

pt.Loon, Belgium: Loon, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lodewijk I van Loon nasceu aproximadamente 1110, seu pai, Arnold II van Loon, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Adelheid, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes de Metz em 1140, em Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de agosto de 1171, em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lodewijk I van Loon родился около 1110 г., его отцу по имени Arnold II van Loon было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Adelheid, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes de Metz в 1140 г. в местности эрфенбач, ландкрис кеисирслаутирн, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 11 августа 1171 г. в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия.

es.Loon, Belgium: Loon, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lodewijk I van Loon nació aproximadamente 1110, su papá, Arnold II van Loon, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Adelheid, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes de Metz en 1140, en Erfenbach, Landkreis Kaiserslautern, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 11 de agosto de 1171, en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica.
